title: Nika Workflow
description: JSON Schema for .nika.yaml workflow files. Validates the semantic YAML format accepted by the Nika workflow engine. Used by nika-lsp and compatible editors (VS Code, Neovim, Zed) for autocomplete and diagnostics.

schema.description: Schema version. Current stable: nika/workflow@0.12. Format: 'nika/workflow@<major>.<minor>'.

name.description: Human-readable workflow name. Shown in TUI and logs.

description.description: Optional free-form description of what this workflow does.

inputs.description: Declared inputs (parameters) for this workflow. Keys are input names, values are input specs.

env.description: Environment variables exposed to exec tasks. String values only.

defaults.description: Default values applied to every task unless overridden per-task.

tasks.description: Ordered list of tasks. Dependency order is resolved via `depends_on` into a DAG.

outputs.description: Named outputs of the workflow, typically templated from task results.

type.description: Expected type for this input. `secret` is redacted from logs.

id.description: Unique task identifier within this workflow. Used in `depends_on` and templates (${tasks.<id>.output}).

verb.description: The 5 Nika verbs: infer (LLM), exec (shell/builtin), fetch (HTTP/file), invoke (MCP tool), agent (multi-turn tool-using LLM).

depends_on.description: Task IDs this task depends on. Forms the execution DAG.

when.description: Conditional expression (template syntax). Task is skipped when this evaluates to false/empty.

for_each.description: Iterate the task body over each element; per-iteration state scoped under `${item}` / `${index}`.

with.description: Verb-specific parameters. Shape depends on `verb`. See https://nika.sh/docs for per-verb reference.

on_error.description: Error handling policy for this task.

output.description: Optional structured-output schema the task result must match.

redact.description: Field paths to redact from logs (useful for secrets/PII).

on.description: Only retry on these error kinds (e.g. 'transient', 'NIKA-503'). Default: all transient errors.
